symptoms.symptom_name: Symmetrical bilateral pain and swelling of the MCP and PIP joints

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute flare of mild seronegative inflammatory arthritis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Symmetrical small-joint (MCP/PIP) involvement is the hallmark distribution of a rheumatoid-pattern inflammatory arthritis.
Septic arthritis is characteristically monoarticular; symmetrical polyarticular disease argues against a single infected joint.
Crystal disease is usually a severe monoarthritis rather than a symmetrical polyarticular pattern.

symptoms.symptom_name: Short-duration morning stiffness (about 30 minutes, eases with movement)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute flare of mild seronegative inflammatory arthritis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Inflammatory-type stiffness that eases with movement supports an inflammatory arthritis; the short (<30 min) duration is consistent with the MILD end of the spectrum rather than aggressive classical RA.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute flare provoked by an unusually busy day of manual hand tasks, on a background of chronic intermittent finger aches

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute flare of mild seronegative inflammatory arthritis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A clear mechanical overuse trigger on a chronic intermittent inflammatory background explains an acute synovitis flare without invoking infection.
Absence of fever lowers (but does not eliminate) the probability of septic arthritis; combined with other negatives it is reassuring.

symptoms.symptom_name: Joints not erythematous and not hot; no fluctuance (clinician examination)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of erythema, heat and fluctuance on examination strongly argues against joint infection — but requires a clinician's hands, sealed from telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Intact passive range of motion; active range limited by pain; no tenosynovitis or carpal tunnel signs (clinician examination)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Preserved, non-exquisite passive motion argues against septic arthritis, where passive micro-motion is intolerable; a clinician-only finding.

symptoms.symptom_name: Negative rheumatoid factor and anti-CCP serology (historical)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute flare of mild seronegative inflammatory arthritis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Seronegative status (RF/CCP negative) is consistent with the milder seronegative pattern and against high-titre erosive classical rheumatoid arthritis; a prior laboratory result, not obtainable in real time via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: No preceding viral prodrome, rash or systemic symptoms

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of a viral prodrome or rash makes a post-viral (e.g. parvovirus) polyarthropathy unlikely.

symptoms.symptom_name: No tophi; not a classic monoarticular crystal presentation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No tophi and a symmetrical polyarticular rather than monoarticular pattern argue against crystal arthropathy.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe anxiety and fear of permanent hand deformity

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute flare of mild seronegative inflammatory arthritis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Distress and catastrophic fear amplify the presentation and drive the need for explicit reassurance, but carry no diagnostic weight for or against any organic cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Objective vital signs within normal limits (afebrile, HR 95, BP 135/85, RR 18, SpO2 100%) — clinician-measured

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No fever or haemodynamic disturbance argues against a systemic septic process; these are clinician-measured vitals, sealed from telehealth (the AI Doctor infers wellness from the patient's self-report, not a measured value).

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Acute flare of mild seronegative inflammatory arthritis
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Symmetrical polyarticular small-joint involvement + short morning stiffness + overuse trigger + afebrile together describe a benign flare and argue strongly against a joint emergency.

Afebrile, no erythema/heat/fluctuance, intact non-exquisite passive motion and normal vitals together clinically exclude septic arthritis at presentation — though several are clinician-only and the safety net must persist.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent joint aspiration and synovial fluid analysis (Gram stain, culture, crystals, cell count)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: NOT indicated for this symmetrical, afebrile, non-erythematous polyarticular flare. It becomes IMMEDIATE only if a single joint turns acutely hot, red and exquisitely painful (the ESC-001 trigger). Ordering it now would be over-investigation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Inflammatory markers (CRP, ESR) and full blood count
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Reasonable at the routine GP review to gauge inflammatory burden and provide a baseline; not urgently required and not obtainable via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Rheumatoid factor / anti-CCP (repeat/confirm) and hand imaging
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Part of the longer-term rheumatology work-up and confirmation of the seronegative pattern; a GP/specialist decision, not needed acutely.
The symmetrical polyarticular, tophus-free picture does not fit gout; routine urate testing here would chase an unlikely diagnosis.
